name: and-the-band-played-on
description: Randy Shilts's 'And the Band Played On: Politics, People, and the AIDS Epidemic' — the landmark investigative history of the early AIDS crisis, exposing how political indifference, bureaucratic infighting, homophobia, and scientific rivalries allowed a deadly epidemic to spiral out of control.
useCase: Understand the early AIDS epidemic, the government failures that allowed it to spread, the community response that saved lives, and the enduring lessons for public health emergencies.
knownRisks: Contains graphic descriptions of illness and death
The Patient Zero narrative has been scientifically contested
Strongly critical of government and scientific institutions
Written in 1987 — does not cover later treatment developments
